howItWorks.title: How the TikTok Lyric Video Generator Works

steps.title: Upload your song
steps.description: Drop an MP3, WAV, or FLAC — or paste a link. No account needed to start. The TikTok lyric video generator works from audio, so you do not need to type a single lyric line. Pick the 15-to-30-second section that will stop the scroll, or use the full track.

steps.title: AI transcribes and syncs every line
steps.description: Lyrics are transcribed automatically from the audio and synced to the beat — in 50+ languages. Click any line to edit. The TikTok lyric video maker positions text inside the vertical safe zone so your words are never buried under TikTok's like, comment, and share buttons.

steps.title: Preview vertically and export in 9:16
steps.description: Watch the draft on a phone-sized canvas before you post. Confirm the first lyric lands within 2 seconds, text is large enough for a 6-inch screen, and nothing is hidden by TikTok UI. Export in 9:16 HD — the same file works on TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.

whyChoose.title: Why Make TikTok Lyric Videos on LyricVideoMaker
whyChoose.description: Most lyric video tools are built for YouTube — horizontal, full-length, desktop-first. LyricVideoMaker is the TikTok lyric video generator built for vertical, short-form, phone-first content from the start.

highlight.title: Vertical-first, not vertical-as-an-afterthought
highlight.description: Every preview, every text layout, and every export is designed around a 9:16 phone screen with TikTok safe zones. You are not cropping a horizontal video — you are building a vertical one from scratch.

cards.title: Phone-screen lyric sizing
cards.description: Lyrics are sized for a vertical phone feed, not squeezed down from a widescreen lyric video. The result stays readable when someone watches with one thumb on the screen.

cards.title: TikTok safe zones built in
cards.description: Lyrics are automatically positioned away from the like, comment, share buttons, and caption area. No more guessing whether your text will be readable after posting.

cards.title: Short-form pacing by default
cards.description: The first lyric appears fast, hook clips stay tight, and endings feel loop-friendly. The page is built for TikTok behavior instead of full-song playback only.

items.question: How long should a TikTok lyric video be?
items.answer: 15 to 30 seconds performs best. Pick the hook, chorus, or the lyric people would repeat after hearing it once, and set start and end points before exporting.

items.question: How to add song lyrics to TikTok video without typing them?
items.answer: Upload your audio to this TikTok lyric video generator. The AI transcribes and syncs every lyric line automatically — no manual typing needed, in 50+ languages.

items.question: Will the lyrics avoid TikTok's buttons and captions?
items.answer: Yes. All TikTok exports keep lyrics inside the safe zone, away from the like, comment, and share buttons and the caption area.

items.question: Can I post the same video to Reels and Shorts?
items.answer: Yes. The 9:16 export works on TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts. One render, three platforms.

items.question: Is this TikTok lyric video maker free?
items.answer: Yes. Sign up, get free credits, and export your TikTok lyric video — no subscription required and no credit card required.

items.question: Can I use TikTok lyric videos commercially?
items.answer: Yes. Your TikTok lyric videos are yours, including monetized and brand accounts. Just make sure you have the rights to the music you upload.

contentSections.heading: Why vertical lyric videos win on TikTok
body: TikTok is a full-screen, sound-on, phone-first feed, and a horizontal lyric video shrunk into that space wastes most of the screen. A vertical 9:16 lyric video fills the frame, puts the words at eye level, and reads instantly with one thumb on the screen — which is exactly how people scroll.

The TikTok lyric video generator builds vertical from the start instead of cropping a widescreen video down. The lyrics are sized for a phone, the motion is tuned for short attention spans, and the hook lands fast enough to stop the scroll before someone swipes away.

contentSections.heading: TikTok safe zones, explained
body: On TikTok, the right edge holds the like, comment, share, and profile buttons, and the bottom holds the caption and username. Anything you place there gets covered. The generator keeps your lyrics inside the safe zone automatically, so the words stay fully visible after the app's interface loads on top of your video.
That means no more exporting a clip, posting it, and discovering your best line is hidden behind the share button. What you preview is what your audience actually reads in the feed.

contentSections.heading: The best length for a TikTok lyric video
body: Short wins on TikTok. A 15 to 30 second clip built around the catchiest line usually outperforms a full song, because it gets to the hook fast and loops cleanly. Pick the chorus or the one line people would repeat after a single listen, and set your start and end points around it.
The first two seconds matter most. The generator gets your first lyric on screen quickly so a scroller sees words moving with the music immediately — the signal that there is something to stay for.

contentSections.heading: One render for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts
body: Vertical 9:16 is the shared format across TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts, so a single export from the TikTok lyric video generator posts natively to all three. You do not re-edit or re-crop for each platform — one render, three feeds.
For a release, that turns one hook into a repeatable short-form routine: cut several lyric moments from the same song and post a fresh vertical clip across every platform without rebuilding anything.

contentSections.heading: Making lyrics readable on a phone screen
body: Readability is the whole game in a feed. Lyrics squeezed down from a widescreen layout end up too small to catch at a glance, so the generator sizes type for a vertical phone screen and keeps one or two lines on screen at a time instead of a wall of text.
Combined with beat-synced timing, that keeps the words easy to follow even when someone is watching with the volume low or half-distracted — the normal state of a scrolling viewer.

contentSections.heading: How to add song lyrics to a TikTok video without typing
body: Upload your track and the TikTok lyric video generator transcribes every line automatically, in 50+ languages, then syncs it to the beat inside a vertical frame. You never paste a lyrics document or drag text on a timeline — the words come from the audio itself.

From there you trim to the section you want, fix any line the AI misheard, confirm nothing sits under the TikTok buttons, and export. A scroll-stopping vertical lyric clip goes from upload to ready in a couple of minutes.

contentSections.heading: Make a lyric clip that loops
body: The best short-form lyric clips feel endless — the end flows back into the start so viewers watch twice without noticing. Choosing a self-contained line or a clean phrase as your start and end points makes a clip loop naturally, which quietly boosts watch time.

Pick the most repeatable moment of the song, keep it tight at 15 to 30 seconds, and let the beat-synced lyrics carry it. A loop-friendly hook clip is often what turns one line into a sound people reuse.
